name: RABBIT-CAGE-BACKLOG-1
title: Extend /rabbit-config to manage permissions; replace scope-guard CLI options with status-file state; route overrides through skill
description: Three related enhancements to rabbit-cage configuration and scope-guard UX: (1) Extend /rabbit-config to manage Claude Code permission entries in settings.json — add subcommands for allowed tools (e.g. create-file, bash), bash command allowlist entries (touch, cat, echo, ls, python), so operators can configure permissions via /rabbit-config rather than hand-editing settings.json. (2) Replace scope-guard command-line option interface with a status-file protocol — scope-guard.sh currently reads .rabbit-scope-override to decide guard state; extend this so the guard state is fully determined by status files (no CLI flags needed), making the interface simpler and more inspectable. (3) Route all one-time and session override requests through the rabbit-feature-touch (or a dedicated) skill instead of having the main session call scope-guard directly with options — the skill handles the confirm-token flow, writes the appropriate status file, and the guard reads it. This removes the option-passing surface and makes override approval an auditable skill invocation.

history.note: TDD cycle complete — implemented /rabbit-config allowed-tools and bash-allow subcommands; 16/16 new tests pass, 6/6 existing tests pass
